text:
Professor: Albert Duncan
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/67247
Review 1:
Some students reported that points were deducted from assignments without explanation and that feedback was limited.
Review 2:
Many students described ECO 201 as relatively easy. Weekly work often consisted of one discussion post, one response to classmates, and one assignment.
Review 3:
Several reviews emphasized that submitting work on time is important because assignments may close after the due date.
Review 4:
Students in asynchronous sections reported there were no exams and that grading criteria were clear.
Review 5:
Many students stated that reading the textbook and PowerPoint materials was necessary for success in the course.
Review 6:
Some students described Professor Duncan as caring,

t reading the textbook and PowerPoint materials was necessary for success in the course.
Review 6:
Some students described Professor Duncan as caring, understanding, and willing to help students facing personal challenges.
Review 7:
Students frequently mentioned that attendance and participation mattered in in-person sections.
Review 8:
Several reviews noted that lectures could feel lecture-heavy or boring, although course expectations were straightforward.
Review 9:
Many students said the course was easy to pass if assignments were completed consistently and on time.
Review 10:
Students often described grading criteria as clear and predictable.
Review 11:
Some students noted that feedback on assignments was limited and that much of the course depended on following instructions in the

ictable.
Review 11:
Some students noted that feedback on assignments was limited and that much of the course depended on following instructions in the syllabus.
Review 12:
Overall reviews suggest that Professor Duncan is organized, fair, and manageable, though experiences varied regarding feedback and lecture quality.

Professor: Angela Polite
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2445910

Review 1:
Students consistently describe Professor Polite as kind, caring, and supportive. Many say she genuinely wants her students to succeed.

Review 2:
Several reviews mention that assignments are straightforward and that earning a high grade is achievable if students complete the required work.

Review 3:
Students frequently praise her ability to explain concepts clearly and make lectures enjoyable.

Review 4:
Many reviewers describe the classroom environment as welcoming, comfortable, and encouraging, especially for students who are nervous about public speaking.

Review 5:
Students report that Professor Polite provides helpful feedback on speeches and assignments.

who are nervous about public speaking.

Review 5:
Students report that Professor Polite provides helpful feedback on speeches and assignments.

Review 6:
Several reviews mention that attendance and participation are important parts of the course.

Review 7:
Students often describe her as inspirational and say she offers encouragement that extends beyond course content.

Review 8:
Many reviewers state that the workload is manageable and that there are no unusually difficult assignments.

Review 9:
Students appreciate her clear grading criteria and fair evaluation process.

Review 10:
Some reviews mention speeches, presentations, discussion posts, quizzes, and a PowerPoint project as course requirements.

process.

Review 10:
Some reviews mention speeches, presentations, discussion posts, quizzes, and a PowerPoint project as course requirements.

Review 11:
Students frequently describe her classes as enjoyable, motivating, and engaging.

Review 12:
Overall reviews indicate that Professor Polite is one of the most highly recommended speech professors at BMCC, with many students saying they would gladly take another course with her.

Professor: Brett Sim
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1558787

Review 1:
Students frequently describe Professor Sims as friendly, respectful, and approachable. Many mention that he is willing to help students outside class.

Review 2:
Several reviews highlight his sense of humor and say that his personality makes classes more enjoyable and engaging.

Review 3:
Students often report that completing homework regularly is the key to success in his courses because exam questions are closely related to assigned practice problems.

Review 4:
Many reviews mention opportunities for extra credit through participation and class engagement.

Review 5:
Students generally describe his lectures as clear and effective, especially for difficult mathematics topics such as calculus.

engagement.

Review 5:
Students generally describe his lectures as clear and effective, especially for difficult mathematics topics such as calculus.

Review 6:
Several students say that he explains concepts thoroughly and spends time reviewing homework problems during class.

Review 7:
Some reviews note that lectures can occasionally be difficult to follow, but students who practice independently and use additional resources are able to succeed.

Review 8:
Many students report that he provides feedback, answers questions, and is accessible outside of class.

Review 9:
Several reviews describe the workload as substantial, with regular homework assignments that require consistent effort.

outside of class.

Review 9:
Several reviews describe the workload as substantial, with regular homework assignments that require consistent effort.

Review 10:
Some students praise his grading fairness and flexibility, while a small number of reviews criticize grading policies or exam-heavy assessment methods.

Review 11:
Students often describe him as inspirational, supportive, and one of the strongest math professors at BMCC.

Review 12:
Overall reviews suggest that students who participate, complete homework, and stay engaged in class usually perform well and would recommend taking his courses.

Professor: Elizabeth Whitney
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1919143

eview 1:
Students frequently describe Professor Whitney as caring, accessible, and responsive to emails. Many reviews mention that she helps students when they have questions and provides support throughout the course.

Review 2:
Several students report that her classes are straightforward and relatively easy if assignments are completed on time and instructions are followed carefully.

Review 3:
Many reviews mention weekly discussion boards, reading assignments, and peer responses as regular course requirements.

Review 4:
Students often describe the grading criteria as clear and easy to understand. Reviews commonly state that completing the required work results in a strong grade.

describe the grading criteria as clear and easy to understand. Reviews commonly state that completing the required work results in a strong grade.

Review 5:
Multiple students note that her online and asynchronous courses include media projects, video presentations, or video essays in addition to discussion activities.

Review 6:
Some students mention a significant amount of weekly reading, particularly in courses such as GWS, but still consider the workload manageable.

Review 7:
Several reviews highlight that major assignments are graded leniently when students follow directions and put effort into their work.

Review 8:
Students frequently praise her responsiveness and willingness to answer questions through email, especially in online courses.

their work.

Review 8:
Students frequently praise her responsiveness and willingness to answer questions through email, especially in online courses.

Review 9:
A few reviews criticize the limited amount of feedback provided on assignments or note that grades are sometimes posted slowly.

Review 10:
Many students describe her courses as easy A opportunities because expectations are clearly communicated and grading is forgiving.

Review 11:
Students report that projects often encourage creativity, self-expression, and the use of multimedia tools such as video creation software.

Review 12:
Overall reviews portray Professor Whitney as supportive, organized, approachable, and highly recommended for students seeking a positive learning experience.

Professor: Ivan Retamoso
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2504915
Review 1:
Students describe Professor Retamoso as caring, supportive, and quick to respond to emails. Many say he allows late work and wants students to succeed.
Review 2:
Several students mention that his lectures are clear and easy to follow. Course notes and lecture materials are posted online.
Review 3:
Students report that he provides practice exams and review sessions before tests, helping them prepare effectively.
Review 4:
Many reviews state that attendance is often not required and that course materials are available online for missed classes.
Review 5:
Students frequently mention opportunities to improve grades through exam retakes, assignment revisions, or late submissions.
Review 6:
Reviews

view 5:
Students frequently mention opportunities to improve grades through exam retakes, assignment revisions, or late submissions.
Review 6:
Reviews describe the workload as manageable but note that homework is assigned regularly.
Review 7:
Students praise his organization and clear grading criteria throughout the course.
Review 8:
Several reviewers say he explains difficult calculus concepts in a simple and undrstandable way.
Review 9:
Students report that he is accessible outside class and willing to answer questions during office hours.
Review 10:
Many reviews describe him as one of the best math professors at BMCC and highly recommend taking his courses.
Review 11:
Some students mention that lectures can move quickly during accelerated winter or summer sessions, but recorded

commend taking his courses.
Review 11:
Some students mention that lectures can move quickly during accelerated winter or summer sessions, but recorded materials help them keep up.
Review 12:
A minority of reviews note that they found the lectures difficult to follow or wished there were additional review sessions, though most feedback is positive.

Professor: Jenna Hirsch
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/804692

Review 1:
Students consistently describe Professor Hirsch as passionate about mathematics and deeply committed to student success. Many reviews mention that she genuinely wants students to understand the material and succeed in class.

Review 2:
Several students praise her ability to explain difficult mathematical concepts in a clear and intuitive way. Reviews often mention that complex topics become easier to understand through her teaching style.

Review 3:
Students frequently describe her lectures as engaging, energetic, and informative. Many reviews highlight her enthusiasm and positive classroom atmosphere.

frequently describe her lectures as engaging, energetic, and informative. Many reviews highlight her enthusiasm and positive classroom atmosphere.

Review 4:
A common theme in reviews is that Professor Hirsch is caring, approachable, and responsive to student questions. Students report receiving quick email responses and additional help when needed.

Review 5:
Many reviews mention that attendance and participation are important components of the course. Students are encouraged to stay engaged and ask questions during class.

Review 6:
Several students note that the workload can be heavy, including homework assignments, quizzes, and regular practice. However, most reviews state that the workload helps reinforce learning.

can be heavy, including homework assignments, quizzes, and regular practice. However, most reviews state that the workload helps reinforce learning.

Review 7:
Students often mention that exams can be challenging and that the pace of the course may feel fast at times, especially in higher-level mathematics courses.

Review 8:
Reviews frequently describe Professor Hirsch as patient and willing to explain concepts multiple times until students understand the material.

Review 9:
Some students praise her use of real-life examples, analogies, humor, and creative teaching techniques to make mathematics more approachable.

Review 10:
Several reviews mention study sessions, review opportunities, and extra support before major exams and quizzes.

more approachable.

Review 10:
Several reviews mention study sessions, review opportunities, and extra support before major exams and quizzes.

Review 11:
A small number of reviews mention that the course can be difficult for students who struggle with mathematics or fall behind on assignments.

Review 12:
Overall, students describe Professor Hirsch as one of the strongest mathematics instructors at BMCC, praising her teaching ability, enthusiasm, accessibility, and dedication to student learning.

Professor: Kenneth Levinson
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1499892

Review 1:
Students frequently describe Professor Levinson as knowledgeable, intelligent, and passionate about language, linguistics, and writing. Many reviews mention that his lectures are clear, informative, and engaging.

Review 2:
A common theme across reviews is the heavy reading and writing workload. Students often report weekly essays, discussion posts, lengthy reading assignments, and writing-intensive coursework.

Review 3:
Several students note that Professor Levinson provides detailed feedback on assignments and is willing to help students improve their writing and critical thinking skills.

that Professor Levinson provides detailed feedback on assignments and is willing to help students improve their writing and critical thinking skills.

Review 4:
Many reviews describe him as caring, understanding, and flexible. Students mention that he often allows late work and works with students who fall behind.

Review 5:
Students frequently mention that success in his courses requires strong time management. Reviews warn against procrastination because assignments can accumulate quickly.

Review 6:
Several reviews praise his use of Blackboard and online course materials. Students report that instructions, readings, assignments, and course resources are organized and accessible.

Blackboard and online course materials. Students report that instructions, readings, assignments, and course resources are organized and accessible.

Review 7:
Many students state that exams are fair and manageable if readings and assignments are completed consistently throughout the semester.

Review 8:
Some reviews describe Professor Levinson as intimidating at first, but most students report that he becomes approachable and supportive once they get to know him.

Review 9:
Students often mention that his classes encourage critical thinking, discussion, and active engagement with course materials rather than simple memorization.

often mention that his classes encourage critical thinking, discussion, and active engagement with course materials rather than simple memorization.

Review 10:
Several reviews note that linguistics courses can be challenging due to the amount of reading, writing, and discussion required, but students generally feel they learn a great deal.

Review 11:
A small number of reviews criticize the workload or mention that grades can sometimes feel difficult to understand, especially when detailed grading explanations are not provided.

Review 12:
Overall, students describe Professor Levinson as a caring and effective instructor who challenges students academically while providing substantial support and feedback throughout the course.

Professor: Lara Stapleton
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/376128

Review 1:
Students consistently describe Professor Stapleton as caring, supportive, and dedicated to student success. Many reviews mention that she creates a welcoming classroom environment where students feel comfortable learning and participating.

Review 2:
A common theme across reviews is her detailed and constructive feedback on essays and writing assignments. Students report that her comments help them improve their writing skills throughout the semester.

Review 3:
Many students praise her ability to teach writing fundamentals, including essay structure, source citation, annotation, and analytical writing. Reviews frequently state that they became stronger writers after taking her class.

structure, source citation, annotation, and analytical writing. Reviews frequently state that they became stronger writers after taking her class.

Review 4:
Students often describe Professor Stapleton as passionate about teaching English and genuinely invested in helping students learn. Her enthusiasm is frequently mentioned in reviews.

Review 5:
Several reviews note that attendance, participation, and arriving on time are important expectations in her classes. Students are encouraged to actively engage in discussions and classroom activities.

Review 6:
Many reviews mention a moderate amount of reading, essays, and homework. While the workload can be substantial at times, students generally describe it as manageable when assignments are completed consistently.

homework. While the workload can be substantial at times, students generally describe it as manageable when assignments are completed consistently.

Review 7:
Students frequently praise her fair grading practices and clear expectations. Reviews often mention that grading criteria are transparent and that feedback is provided in a timely manner.

Review 8:
Several students describe Professor Stapleton as approachable, understanding, and willing to help outside of class. Students report that she responds to questions and provides support when needed.

Review 9:
A recurring theme in reviews is that she motivates students who previously disliked English or writing. Many students state that her teaching increased their confidence as writers.

that she motivates students who previously disliked English or writing. Many students state that her teaching increased their confidence as writers.

Review 10:
Students frequently describe her lectures and class discussions as engaging, relevant, and connected to real-life experiences. Readings are often viewed as meaningful and thought-provoking.

Review 11:
Some reviews mention occasional pop quizzes, regular reading assignments, and essay deadlines, requiring students to stay organized throughout the semester.

Review 12:
Overall, students describe Professor Stapleton as one of the strongest English instructors at BMCC, highlighting her feedback, fairness, enthusiasm, and commitment to student growth.

Professor: Louise Yan
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2262999

Review 1:
Students describe Professor Yan as knowledgeable and strong at explaining programming concepts, especially C++ and computer science fundamentals.

Review 2:
Several students say she codes live during lectures instead of only reading from slides. Students who like live coding examples find this helpful for understanding how programs are built.

Review 3:
Many positive reviews say she explains topics clearly, makes sure students understand concepts, and is willing to answer repeated questions.

Review 4:
Some students describe her as organized, clear, kind, and one of their favorite professors at BMCC.

willing to answer repeated questions.

Review 4:
Some students describe her as organized, clear, kind, and one of their favorite professors at BMCC.

Review 5:
Several students say her courses are fast paced and require students to pay close attention during class.

Review 6:
Many reviews mention that exams and quizzes are important parts of the grade. Some students describe the class as test-heavy, with quizzes, a midterm, and a final.

Review 7:
Some students warn that her exams can be difficult, especially because students may need to write full programs or code on paper.

Review 8:
Several negative reviews say the lecture pace is too fast and that students who fall behind may struggle.

full programs or code on paper.

Review 8:
Several negative reviews say the lecture pace is too fast and that students who fall behind may struggle.

Review 9:
Students describe mixed experiences with grading. Some say grading is fair and clear, while others mention tough grading or late grading.

Review 10:
Some reviews mention limited flexibility, such as no retakes, no extra credit, or heavy weight on major exams.

Review 11:
Positive reviews say she genuinely cares about students and wants them to learn the material rather than just pass.

Review 12:
Overall, reviews suggest Professor Yan is a strong computer science instructor for students who are prepared to keep up, attend class, study consistently, and actively practice programming.

Professor: Mohammad Azhar
Source: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1869854

Review 1:
Students frequently describe Professor Azhar as caring, supportive, and invested in student success both inside and outside the classroom.

Review 2:
Many reviews mention that his courses involve a significant workload, especially in honors sections such as CSC211H. Students often report numerous projects, assignments, and independent learning activities.

Review 3:
Several students praise him for creating opportunities beyond the classroom, including research projects, internships, industry networking, innovation challenges, and professional development activities.

beyond the classroom, including research projects, internships, industry networking, innovation challenges, and professional development activities.

Review 4:
Students often describe him as motivational and encouraging. Many reviews state that he believes in students’ potential and pushes them to improve their coding and problem-solving skills.

Review 5:
Multiple reviews mention that he provides flexibility and opportunities for students to complete assignments, extensions, or additional work when needed.

Review 6:
Some students believe the workload is excessive and that the class can become overwhelming, especially for students who are not prepared to dedicate significant time outside class.

is excessive and that the class can become overwhelming, especially for students who are not prepared to dedicate significant time outside class.

Review 7:
Reviews are mixed regarding teaching style. Some students find his explanations helpful and effective, while others feel the course relies heavily on self-study and independent learning.

Review 8:
Many students mention coding projects, group projects, and hands-on assignments that help build practical programming experience and strengthen GitHub portfolios.

Review 9:
Students consistently note that his honors courses are challenging but rewarding. Several reviews mention that the difficulty prepares students for internships, four-year colleges, and professional software development environments.

reviews mention that the difficulty prepares students for internships, four-year colleges, and professional software development environments.

Review 10:
Professor Azhar is often described as accessible outside class and willing to answer questions when students need additional support.

Review 11:
Some negative reviews state that the pace can be difficult to follow and that students who are not confident in coding may struggle with the workload and expectations.

Review 12:
Overall, reviews suggest Professor Azhar is best suited for motivated students who want strong preparation, professional opportunities, and substantial coding experience, even if it requires significant effort and independent learning.
